results.code:
FUNCTION FindFirstNegative( aArray )
    // AScan evaluates the block for each element until .T. is returned.
    // It returns the 1-based index of the first match, or 0 if none found.
    RETURN AScan( aArray, {|x| x < 0} )

FUNCTION Main()
    LOCAL aTest := {5, 3, -2, 8, -1}
    LOCAL nIdx

    nIdx := FindFirstNegative( aTest )

    IF nIdx > 0
        ? "First negative number is at index:", nIdx, ;
          " (value: ", aTest[nIdx], ")"
    ELSE
        ? "No negative numbers found."
    ENDIF

    RETURN NIL

results.code:
FUNCTION MinMax( aArray )
   LOCAL nMin, nMax, i, nLen

   // Handle empty or invalid arrays
   IF Empty( aArray ) OR ( nLen := Len( aArray ) ) == 0
      RETURN { NIL, NIL }
   ENDIF

   // Initialize with first element
   nMin := aArray[ 1 ]
   nMax := aArray[ 1 ]

   // Single pass through the rest of the array
   FOR i := 2 TO nLen
      IF aArray[ i ] < nMin
         nMin := aArray[ i ]
      ENDIF
      IF aArray[ i ] > nMax
         nMax := aArray[ i ]
      ENDIF
   NEXT i

   RETURN { nMin, nMax }
ENDFUNCTION

/**
 * Counts the total number of keys in a Harbour THash object.
 * 
 * @param  oHash  THash instance to inspect
 * @return        Integer count of keys, or 0 if input is invalid
 */
FUNCTION CountHashKeys( oHash )
    LOCAL nCount := 0

    // Validate that the parameter is a THash object
    IF ValType( oHash ) = "O" .AND. ;
       oHash:Class()

results.code:
FUNCTION SelectionSort( aArr )
    LOCAL nLen := LEN( aArr )
    LOCAL i, j, nMinIdx, nTemp

    // Edge case: arrays with 0 or 1 elements are already sorted
    IF nLen <= 1
        RETURN aArr
    ENDIF

    // Iterate over the array, leaving the last element in place
    FOR i := 1 TO nLen - 1
        nMinIdx := i
        
        // Find index of minimum element in the unsorted portion [i+1 .. nLen]
        FOR j := i + 1 TO nLen
            IF aArr[ j ] < aArr[ nMinIdx ]
                nMinIdx := j
            ENDIF
        NEXT

        // Swap the found minimum with the element at position i

results.code:
FUNCTION ModifyValue( @nVal )
    // nVal is passed by reference
    nVal := nVal * 2 + 10
    RETURN NIL
END FUNCTION
LOCAL nMyVar := 5

? "Before: ", nMyVar   // Output: Before: 5
ModifyValue( @nMyVar ) // Note the @ before the variable name
? "After:  ", nMyVar   // Output: After: 20
FUNCTION AppendToArray( @aArr, cItem )
    aAdd( aArr, cItem )
    RETURN NIL
END FUNCTION

LOCAL aList := { "Alpha", "Beta" }
AppendToArray( @aList, "Gamma" )
? aList[3]  // Output: Gamma
